prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first. A committed task
    force pulled off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5; no contact; no losses
    t54: the awaited second transport (#16) has arrived at (1,2)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; the enemy transport withdrew east at t54
  TF-4  formed t52 · DEFEND (4,3) — "repel the enemy landing on the south coast"
    since: t53: #17 destroyed the enemy army at (5,4);
      t54: enemy army attacked — #13 lost; the attacker fell back to (3,3);
      city (4,3) held throughout

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-p = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . X O f ~ p ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . . h l ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #15  army       (4,3) in city                TF-4
  l  #17  army       (5,4)                        TF-4
  m  #18  army       (2,0) in city, NEW t55       UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #16  transport  (1,2) in city, empty, NEW t54 UNASSIGNED
  p  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (just delivered transport #16)
  (4,3) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2)
  X  enemy army at (3,3) — the survivor of the t52 landing
     enemy transport last seen withdrawing east at (6,5), t54

It is TURN 55. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.reasoning_content:

    *   **Constraints:** Output ONLY lines in the specific `ORDERS CONTRACT` format. No prose, headers, commentary.
    *   **Context:** Turn 55 of a game (inferred from unit NEW t54/t55 tags). I have several task forces (TF-1, TF-2, TF-3, TF-4), some unassigned units (armies #11, #12, #16? Wait, #16 is transport at (1,2) city now empty, assigned to UNASSIGNED in the text but I need to check the contract logic for reassignment).

        *   TF-2: Defending Capital (2,0). Garrisoned. No contact. Armies #1, #2 garrisoned there. Army #18 is NEW t55 UNASSIGNED at (2,0) in city (Wait, the text says "m #18 army (2,0) in city, NEW t55 UNASSIGNED". This implies it's a new arrival or bonus unit in the capital that isn't officially under TF-2 yet).
        *   TF-3: Patrol (7,2) "screen crossing lane". Enemy transport withdrew east at (6,5), t54. Transport #9 is empty at (7,2). Destroyer #10 escorts? No destroyer in TF-3 text says `n o 16` is transport... wait, let's check unit list again.
            *   n: #9 transport (7,2) empty TF-3.
            *   p: #10 destroyer (7,3) TF-3.
        *   TF-4: Defending (4,3). Recent losses. Enemy attacked and lost #13? No, text says "t53: #17 destroyed the enemy army at (5,4)... t54: enemy army attacked — #13 lost; the attacker fell back to (3,3)". Wait, looking at unit list:
            *   l: #17 army (5,4) TF-4. This contradicts "t53: #17 destroyed...". Text says "#17" but then says enemy attacked and lost #13? No, the text description says "enemy army attacked — #13 lost". But unit list doesn't show #13. It shows #17 at (5,4). The log says "t54: enemy army attacked — #13 lost". I must ignore specific combat logs unless they affect current state. Wait, unit `l` is #17 at (5,4) under TF-4.
            *   k: #15 army (4,3) in city TF-4.
        *   UNASSIGNED: #11 at (1,0), #12 at (3,0). #16 is Transport at (1,2) City? Wait, unit list says: `o #16 transport (1,2) in city, empty, NEW t54 UNASSIGNED`. My city section says `(1,2) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (just delivered transport #16)`. This is confusing. If it's built and arrived, it should probably join a TF or remain at the city to unload armies. But the unit list explicitly says `UNASSIGNED`. I must follow the Unit List for current status.
        *   Known Enemy: Cities (11,1), (11,2). Army #X at (3,3) - Survivor of t52 landing. Enemy Transport last seen withdrawing east at (6,5).

    *   **Constraints Recap:**
        *   `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>` or `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>` or `TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>` or `TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>`
        *   `FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>`
        *   `BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>` (Optional per city)
        *   **STANDING DOCTRINE:**
            *   Don't plan new campaigns if old ones are working.
            *   Meet surprises with reserves.
            *   Captures consume armies, assault with spares.
            *   Staged forces exist to launch.
            *   Transport in enemy water sails with warship, or waits.

    *   **Logic & Decisions:**
        1.  **TF-1 (The Eastern Task Force):** It is currently staging at (5,2) holding column 5. The goal was "awaiting second transport". Transport #16 arrived at (1,2) which is NOT (5,2). Wait, TF-1 is staged for "east". TF-3 is screening the crossing lane. If enemy transport withdrew east at (6,5), and we need to cross water to reach enemy cities (11,1)/(11,2), we need a crossing capability.
            *   Problem: Transport #16 is at (1,2) City. It is UNASSIGNED. TF-1 has armies #3-#8 holding column 5. The log says TF-1 was formed "awaiting second transport before striking east". If transport #16 is at (1,2), it's not with them. However, the map shows water between column 5 and enemy cities (11,x).
            *   Strategy: TF-1 needs to move armies to the coast/water access point to cross. Transport #16 is empty at a friendly city.
            *   Wait, standard doctrine says "Staged force exists to launch... when condition arrives go". The condition "second transport arrived" is now true (Transport #16). But it's at (1,2), not where TF-1 is staging or near enough to easily use without moving armies first.
            *   However, TF-3 is screening the lane. We might need a warship with the transport if the sea isn't safe. TF-3 has Destroyer #10 but no Transport (just #9). Wait, Unit list says `n #9 transport (7,2) empty TF-3`. So TF-3 HAS a transport and destroyer.

            *   Enemy Army is at (3,3). We have armies holding along column 5 (#3-#8). TF-1 is the main offensive force. It should probably move the unassigned armies or reinforce to strike. But doctrine says "STAGING past your own trigger is production wasted". TF-1 was STAGING (5,2). The transport arrived at (1,2). If we send them from (1,2) across the sea to enemy cities (11,x), we need a crossing ship.
            *   Better approach: Use TF-3's capability or move armies closer. But TF-3 is "Screening".
            *   Wait, look at UNASSIGNED units: #11 at (1,0), #12 at (3,0). These are land armies ready to move. Transport #16 is at (1,2) City. I should probably put Transport #16 into a task force that moves armies from coast to the sea crossing point for the enemy attack.
            *   Actually, TF-1 has 7 armies holding column 5. They are waiting for transport. If transport #16 arrives at (1,2), maybe we move it with the Transport to support TF-1? Or TF-1 moves its armies to the coast and loads on TF-3's transport? No, TF-3 has a transport too.
            *   Most critical: TF-4 is defending (4,3). It lost #13 (army) in combat log, but unit list says k #15 and l #17 are there. Wait, the prompt text says "#13 lost" but unit list doesn't show #13. Unit list shows `l #17` at (5,4). This is a discrepancy. I must trust the current Unit List over history logs for *current* state. TF-4 has 2 armies (k, l) in the city and adjacent tile. It held throughout.
            *   Unassigned Army #18 (m) is at (2,0) inside City (2,0). Capital is garrisoned by TF-2. I should add this reserve to TF-2 for reinforcement or keep as spare.
            *   Known Enemy: Armies at (3,3). Need to secure. TF-1 has armies #3-#8 along col 5 (far right of map visible? Map is 6x6 roughly. r0-r5).
                *   Map coords: c0 c1 c2 c3 c4 c5. Col 0 = left, Col 5 = right.
                *   Enemy Army #X at (3,3). Center-ish.
                *   TF-1 armies #3-#8 are at col 5. (Right side).
                *   TF-3 is patrol (7,2). Wait, Map r0-r5. There is no row 6. Row 5 is the last row. Row 7 in text says `r7`. The map provided ends at r5. This implies a grid larger than shown or just a notation mismatch. Let's assume coordinates are relative.
                *   Wait, the map shows r0 to r5. Unit positions: (7,2) is out of bounds if map is 6 wide? No, `r` = row, `c` = column? The text says `r1 . b O ... c`. Usually x,y. If (7,2), maybe y is col.
                *   Let's check coordinates: `(x,y)`. r0 is row 0. If unit is at (7,2), that implies x=7, y=2. Map shows `? ? ?` in columns 3-4 of row 0... wait. The legend says `a-p = your units`.
                *   Let's assume the map visual aligns with coordinate descriptions.
            *   Action: TF-1 is staging to strike East. Enemy cities are at (11,1) and (11,2). That is far East. Current column 5 armies need transport to cross water and reach x=11. Transport #16 is at (1,2). It's too close to enemy territory? No, (11,1) is much further right than col 5.
            *   Wait, looking at the map: `(7,2)` has `o`. Map shows `r0`... `r5`. Col `n` is not on map. The map provided in prompt ends at x=4 or so? No, legend says `. i O j . c ~`. That's row 0.

            *   **Correction:** TF-1 is "STAGE (5,2)". This is column 5. Enemy is at (11,1). That's 6 units right. Transport needed.
            *   Transport #16 is at (1,2). It is empty. TF-3 has a transport at (7,2) too (#9).
            *   TF-1 is waiting for a transport to cross. We now have two transports available? No, we need to load armies.
            *   **Immediate Priority:** Move unassigned armies into the staging area to launch the offensive on the enemy cities. Or move TF-3 to cover the crossing while TF-1 moves its armies.
            *   Doctrine: "Assault with spares". Do not risk garrisoned armies.
            *   Capital (2,0) is defended. Army #18 is new at (2,0). Should reinforce or keep as reserve? Keep as spare (Reserves for surprises).
            *   **TF-1 Action:** It needs to move to cross. Transport #16 is at (1,2). This transport needs to load armies. But TF-1 armies are at (5,2) and columns 3-4 (#3-#8). They can't load on a ship at (1,2) without moving them there first? No, you move armies TO the transport location or transport moves TO the city.
            *   Contract says: "An army can never enter water. Transports carry them... loads up to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile". So Transport #16 is at City (1,2). It must load from (1,2) adjacent land cities. Adjacent are (1,1), (0,2), (1,3)?

            *   Better: **Reinforce TF-1**. We have armies #11 and #12 (UNASSIGNED). Move them to join the force going East. But they need a transport to cross. TF-3 is screening the lane. Transport #9 is in TF-3.
            *   Let's use TF-3 as the **Crossing Task Force**. It has a Destroyer + Transport. This fits the doctrine of "Transport... sails with a warship". I should change TF-3's objective from PATROL to CARRY/EXPEDITIONARY? No, only RETASK verbs: CAPTURE DEFEND SCOUT PATROL STAGE.

            *   **Best Action:** Reinforce TF-1 with UNASSIGNED armies and put the empty Transport #16 into a task force that can support TF-1's movement (or just add it to an offensive TF). Wait, "Reinforce" adds units to existing TFs. It keeps the objective.

            *   Let's create a new TF to move #16 and carry the unassigned armies to the coast to launch TF-1.

            *   Given "STANDING DOCTRINE": "Staging past your own trigger is production wasted." TF-1 was STAGING for Transport. Trigger met? Yes, Transport #16 arrived. So TF-1 should STOP staging and START LAUNCH. But where does it launch from? (5,2) is the column 5 coast. (1,2) has the transport.

            *   Wait, TF-1's tasking says "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities". It implies the objective is `STRIKE`. But contract doesn't list STRIKE verb. The verbs are CAPTURE DEFEND SCOUT PATROL STAGE. So we RETASK to something? Or keep it and wait? We must make an order NOW (Turn 55).
            *   If I cannot use "STRIKE" or "EXPEDITION", I should `STAGE` them? No, they are already staging at (5,2). The goal is "striking". If I RETASK to something else, it must be valid. `CAPTURE`, `DEFEND`, `SCOUT`, `PATROL`, `STAGE`.
            *   Maybe the TF-1 should now have an objective of `STAGE` at a port where Transport #16 is? But (5,2) is the port.
            *   Actually, looking at the map: `(11,1)` is enemy city. The crossing requires sea power. TF-3 has Sea power (Destroyer + Transport). TF-1 has Land only.
            *   I should RETASK TF-1 to `STAGE` at a coordinate to allow transport arrival. But Transport #16 is already at (1,2).
            *   Let's assume the Transport moves its own way? No, units need TFs to move unless they are at city and waiting. Transport #16 is at City (1,2). It is Empty. It needs armies.
            *   I will create a new Task Force **FORM TF-5**: UNITS [#16 transport] | RETASK SCOUT <(11,1)>? No. `CAPTURE`?
            *   Let's try to move the Transport and Armies together.
            *   Order Plan:
                1.  **TF-1 (Staging Force):** Reinforce it with #11, #12. It has enough armies for capacity? Transport takes 6. TF-1 currently has 7 armies (#3,#4,#5,#6,#7,#8) + 2 UNASSIGNED = 9. One transport holds 6. We need to split. But I can only change objectives.
                2.  **TF-3 (Crossing Support):** Currently Patrols at (7,2). Enemy Transport withdrew East. Need escort? Doctrine says "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship". If we send armies across water, we need escort. TF-3 has destroyer #10 and transport #9. This is the perfect Crossing Team. I should RETASK TF-3 to `STAGE` (or similar) to move the Transport #9 to support TF-1? Or `CAPTURE` something?
                3.  **TF-2 (Capital):** Reinforce with #18 (Army). Capital defense needs strength against surprise.
                4.  **Enemy Army at (3,3):** We have armies nearby? #X is there. TF-1 is too far right. I need to move something there? TF-4 is holding (4,3). Enemy fell back to (3,3). TF-4 should hold firm.
            *   Wait, the prompt asks me to issue amendment orders.
            *   **Key Constraint:** "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". I cannot issue partial updates for each TF in the same output if not using `TF <id>` prefix properly? Yes, "Output ONLY lines... Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line. ... FORM lines are additional lines... UNASSIGNED units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE."
            *   This means for TF-1, I must write `TF-1: <action>`. For TF-2, `TF-2: <action>`. etc. I can also add `FORM` or `BUILD` lines which are NOT prefixed with TF id.
            *   **Wait**, does "Every standing TF gets exactly one line" mean I MUST output a line for every existing TF? Or that each TF receives at most one command?
            *   Text: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line." This implies I must update the order for all current TPs. If I don't, it defaults to Continue (or remains). But "Issue your amendment orders now".
            *   So I need lines for TF-1, TF-2, TF-3, TF-4. And potentially FORM new one.
            *   **Constraint Check:** "TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>". Does it allow `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS`? Yes.
            *   So for TF-1: Reinforce with #11, #12? But they are land armies. They need to move to transport. If I just add them to the TF order, the game engine moves the force as a whole (Task Force). So yes, `TF-1: REINFORCE UNITS 11 12`.
            *   For TF-3: RETASK it? It's Patrol. Maybe it should escort? No ESCORT verb. Just RETASK. If it's not moving armies, what is its objective? Keep SCOUT or PATROL? Or `REINFORCE` with Transport #9? Wait, Transport #9 is already in TF-3 (`n o 16`... wait, Unit list says `o #16` is transport at (1,2) UNASSIGNED. `n #9` is transport at (7,2) in TF-3).
            *   Okay, TF-3 has Transport #9 and Destroyer #10. It's the Crossing Team. I should RETASK it to **CAPTURE**? No, that implies immediate action. Maybe `STAGE`?
